nodes.label: CYC Survey Platform
nodes.rationale: A multilingual survey platform with admin create/edit/results pages and respondent-facing survey pages, supporting English, French, and Chinese.

nodes.label: npm run dev
nodes.rationale: The development server command serving the Next.js frontend on localhost:3000.

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph (graphify)
nodes.rationale: A persistent knowledge graph of the codebase stored in graphify-out/ with god nodes, community detection, and cross-file relationships, enabling quick structural queries.

nodes.label: graphify query CLI
nodes.rationale: A CLI command returning a scoped subgraph for codebase questions, much smaller than raw GREP or GRAPH_REPORT.md.

nodes.label: graphify path CLI
nodes.rationale: A CLI command for discovering relationships between two concepts in the knowledge graph.

nodes.label: graphify explain CLI
nodes.rationale: A CLI command for focused concept exploration within the knowledge graph.

nodes.label: graphify update CLI
nodes.rationale: AST-only incremental update of the knowledge graph after code modifications, incurring no API cost.

nodes.label: graphify-out/
nodes.rationale: The output directory containing the serialized knowledge graph, wiki index, and GRAPH_REPORT.md.

nodes.label: GRAPH_REPORT.md
nodes.rationale: A broad architecture review document generated from the knowledge graph; used only when query/path/explain do not surface enough context.

nodes.label: PDF Translation Upload
nodes.rationale: Admin users can upload a PDF of pre-translated survey questions to auto-populate translation fields, avoiding manual typing for large surveys. The translation is saved to ai_analyses for review before final save.

nodes.label: POST /api/surveys/{survey_id}/translation/upload
nodes.rationale: New FastAPI endpoint accepting multipart form data (PDF file + language parameter), extracting text via pdfplumber, mapping to question UUIDs via Gemini, and upserting into ai_analyses.

nodes.label: pdfplumber
nodes.rationale: Python library for extracting text from PDFs. Chosen over other PDF libraries because it provides clean text extraction suitable for LLM ingestion.

nodes.label: Google Gemini API
nodes.rationale: Used for structured JSON extraction of translated survey content from raw PDF text. Chosen for its JSON mode with low temperature for deterministic output mapping.

nodes.label: gemini-3.5-flash
nodes.rationale: The specific Gemini model used for translation extraction, selected for fast response times and structured JSON output capability.

nodes.label: Gemini Prompt Strategy
nodes.rationale: The prompt includes English reference questions with indices and types, the raw PDF text, and strict JSON schema instructions. Question mapping is position-based (by section+index) rather than text matching to avoid fragility across languages.

nodes.label: ai_analyses Table
nodes.rationale: Supabase table storing AI-generated analysis results including translations. Uses analysis_type key (translation_fr, translation_zh) to namespace different AI outputs per survey. No new DB migration needed — reuses existing schema.

nodes.label: Translation Mode UI
nodes.rationale: A blue info banner on the admin edit page visible when language is not English, showing an upload PDF button alongside the warning that structural changes are disabled.

nodes.label: Question Mapping by Position Index
nodes.rationale: Translations are mapped to question UUIDs by 0-based index position within survey sections, relying on PDF questions appearing in the same order as the reference. Avoids fragile text matching across languages.

nodes.label: PDF Text Extraction
nodes.rationale: Raw text is extracted from all PDF pages using pdfplumber before being sent to Gemini. No OCR or layout analysis needed — plain text extraction is sufficient for LLM-based structured parsing.

nodes.label: Translation Error Handling
nodes.rationale: Errors are categorized: non-PDF files (400), oversized files (413), Gemini failures (502), invalid JSON (502), mismatched question counts (partial mapping), unmatched sections (unmatched key). Graceful degradation ensures admin can still review and fix.

nodes.label: PDF Upload Data Flow
nodes.rationale: Admin uploads PDF → Backend extracts text → Gemini parses JSON → Maps indices to UUIDs → Upserts ai_analyses → Frontend re-fetches translations → Populates edit fields → Admin reviews and saves. Two-step save allows admin verification before persistence.

nodes.label: Full Survey Questions.pdf
nodes.rationale: The reference PDF containing the full set of translated survey questions, used for end-to-end testing of the PDF translation upload feature.

nodes.label: Short Answer Validation
nodes.rationale: Adds configurable validation to short_answer question type with preset options (postal_code_prefix, email, custom regex) and an optional description/helper-text field for all question types. Immediate use case is postal code prefix demographic collection.

nodes.label: Validation Presets
nodes.rationale: Pre-configured validation rules (none, email, postal_code_prefix, regex) with auto-filled fields, balancing ease-of-use with flexibility. Only the 'regex' type exposes manual fields to admins.

nodes.label: Postal Code Prefix (A1A)
nodes.rationale: A validation preset enforcing max 3 characters matching ^[A-Z][0-9][A-Z]$ with automatic uppercase normalization. Collects the first three characters of a Canadian postal code for demographic analysis without collecting full identifiable addresses.

nodes.label: Email Validation
nodes.rationale: A validation preset with regex ^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$ and max length of 254 characters. Provides standard email format checking.

nodes.label: Custom Regex Validation
nodes.rationale: Admins can define their own regex pattern, max length, and uppercase normalization for arbitrary validation needs not covered by presets.

nodes.label: Question Description Field
nodes.rationale: An optional helper-text/description field available on all question types. Stores context like 'We ask for the first three characters of your postal code to get a general sense of where responses are coming from.' Rendered below question text in muted, italic styling.

nodes.label: Validation Config
nodes.rationale: JSON object stored in the question's options JSONB column with fields: type, regex, max_length, normalize_uppercase. Extends existing per-type configuration pattern without schema migration.

nodes.label: Normalize Uppercase
nodes.rationale: Both client-side (on input change) and server-side (on save) normalization to uppercase for postal codes. Ensures consistent data format regardless of how users type their input.

nodes.label: Client + Server Validation
nodes.rationale: Validation runs both in the browser (before advancing to the next question) and on the server (PUT /api/sessions/{id}/answers). Client-side prevents bad data from being submitted; server-side prevents data quality issues at the persistence boundary.

nodes.label: PUT /api/sessions/{session_id}/answers
nodes.rationale: Existing auto-save endpoint for answers, extended with server-side validation logic that checks regex, max_length, and applies uppercase normalization before writing to the answers table.

nodes.label: Short Answer Respondent UI Flow
nodes.rationale: Renders an <input> with browser-native maxLength for validated questions (textarea for unvalidated). On step advance: normalizes to uppercase, validates against regex, shows inline error if failed, saves via PUT on success. Optional questions skip validation when empty.

nodes.label: Validation Error Translations
nodes.rationale: Error messages are translated to all three platform languages (en/fr/zh) via LanguageContext. Client-side only — server-side validation errors are not i18n'd, which is listed as out of scope.

nodes.label: test_short_answer_validation.py
nodes.rationale: Standalone Python test file covering valid postal codes, lowercase/mixed-case normalization, invalid formats, max length enforcement, empty required/optional handling, and validation config round-trip.

nodes.label: Admin Create Page
nodes.rationale: The page at src/app/admin/create/page.tsx where admins compose new surveys. Extended with description field for all question types and validation config dropdown for short_answer questions.

nodes.label: Admin Results Page
nodes.rationale: The page at src/app/admin/results/[id]/page.tsx showing survey response summaries. Extended to display question descriptions in both summary and individual response tabs.

nodes.label: Respondent Survey Page
nodes.rationale: The page at src/app/survey/[id]/page.tsx where respondents fill out surveys. Extended with description rendering, validation-aware input (maxLength, uppercase normalization), and client-side validation on step advance.

nodes.label: options JSONB
nodes.rationale: The JSONB column on the questions table storing per-type configuration. Its polymorphic nature (choices for MC, validation for short_answer, description for all types) avoids schema migrations — any shape is accepted by PostgreSQL JSONB.

nodes.label: Question Types
nodes.rationale: Supported question types: multiple_choice, short_answer, rating_scale, likert_scale, section_header, checkbox, dropdown, ranking. Each type stores its configuration in the polymorphic options JSONB column.

nodes.label: Supabase (PostgreSQL/PostgREST)
nodes.rationale: The backend database platform providing PostgreSQL storage with PostgREST auto-generated API. All survey data, questions, answers, and AI analyses are stored here.

nodes.label: FastAPI Backend
nodes.rationale: Python web framework hosting the API at api/index.py. Provides REST endpoints for survey CRUD, session management, answer storage, and AI-powered features.

nodes.label: Next.js Frontend
nodes.rationale: TypeScript React frontend using Next.js with Tailwind CSS. Pages for admin create/edit/results and respondent-facing survey experience.

nodes.label: Tailwind CSS
nodes.rationale: CSS utility framework used for all styling across the platform, including dark mode support via the dark: prefix variant.

nodes.label: Gemini Temperature 0.1
nodes.rationale: Low temperature setting for Gemini calls ensures deterministic, consistent JSON output when mapping PDF text to structured survey translations.

nodes.label: File Validation (PDF)
nodes.rationale: Upload endpoint validates file extension (.pdf), MIME type, and file size (max 10MB). Early rejection prevents unnecessary processing and provides clear error feedback.

nodes.label: VALIDATION_PRESETS
nodes.rationale: A TypeScript constant mapping preset names to their regex, max_length, and normalize_uppercase values. Used on both admin create and edit pages to auto-fill validation fields when a preset is selected.

nodes.label: updateValidationType Function
nodes.rationale: React state handler that updates question validation config when admin selects a preset. For non-regex presets, auto-fills regex/max_length/normalize fields.

nodes.label: QuestionDraft Interface
nodes.rationale: TypeScript interface representing a question in the admin editor. Extended with question_description, validation_type, validation_regex, validation_max_length, and validation_normalize_uppercase fields.

nodes.label: Browser Native maxLength
nodes.rationale: Uses HTML <input maxLength={3}> to provide browser-native length enforcement as a first line of defense before programmatic validation.

nodes.label: No DB Migration Required
nodes.rationale: Both features leverage existing JSONB columns rather than adding new schema columns. The question_type enum already includes short_answer, and options JSONB accepts arbitrary shapes. This avoids migration risks and deployment coordination.

nodes.label: Out of Scope (Short Answer Validation)
nodes.rationale: CSV/Excel export, validation presets beyond email and postal code, server-side i18n for error messages (client-side only), and realtime keystroke validation. These are deferred to keep scope focused on the immediate postal code use case.

nodes.label: Structural Changes Disabled in Translation Mode
nodes.rationale: Adding, deleting, or reordering questions and options is blocked when the admin is editing in a non-English language. Prevents inconsistencies between the English reference structure and its translations.
nodes.norm_label: structural changes disabled in translation mode

nodes.label: Rich Text in Translations
nodes.rationale: Translated question text may contain HTML markup like <strong>, <em> tags, preserving formatting from the original English content in other languages.

nodes.label: Polymorphic Options Pattern
nodes.rationale: The options JSONB column stores different shapes per question type: {choices: [...]} for MC/checkbox/dropdown/ranking, {description: '...'} for section_header, {validation: {...}} for short_answer. This polymorphic pattern eliminates the need for separate option tables per type.

nodes.label: logo.png
nodes.summary: CYC brand logo image used in the header/footer layout component as the site branding element.
nodes.details: PNG file served as a static asset. Referenced in HeaderFooter.tsx (line 32) as to display the CYC logo in the site header. Serves as the visual brand identifier for the CYC Survey Platform.

nodes.label: CYC_Logo.png
nodes.summary: Full CYC logo used in the splash/intro animation on the homepage.
nodes.details: PNG file displayed during the intro state on the homepage (page.tsx line 272). Larger/more prominent version of the CYC brand logo shown as part of the landing page splash screen.

nodes.label: public/vercel.svg
nodes.description: Vercel deployment platform logo SVG (triangle/arrow mark). Represents the Vercel hosting platform used to deploy this Next.js application. The project is deployed at cyc-survey-platform.vercel.app and uses vercel.json for build/rewrite/cron configuration.

nodes.label: public/next.svg
nodes.description: Next.js framework wordmark SVG logo. Represents the Next.js framework — the core frontend technology of this project providing React-based SSR, App Router, and file-based routing.

nodes.label: public/globe.svg
nodes.description: 16x16 globe icon SVG with meridians and parallels. A web/global-connectivity UI metaphor. Default create-next-app scaffold icon usable for Open Graph images, favicons, or web-related UI decoration.

nodes.label: public/window.svg
nodes.description: 16x16 browser-window icon SVG with three title-bar dots. A desktop-application UI metaphor. Default create-next-app scaffold icon usable for dashboard/app-related UI decoration or Open Graph images.

nodes.label: Vercel Platform
nodes.description: Cloud deployment and hosting platform for frontend frameworks. This project is deployed on Vercel, configured via vercel.json with build command, install command, Rewrites for Python API proxy, and Cron Jobs for scheduled reminder emails.

nodes.label: Next.js Framework
nodes.description: React-based full-stack web framework providing server-side rendering, static generation, App Router, API routes, and optimized image/build tooling. The foundation technology of this survey platform.

nodes.label: create-next-app Scaffold
nodes.description: The official Next.js project bootstrap tool. These four SVG files (vercel.svg, next.svg, globe.svg, window.svg) are default scaffold assets shipped with create-next-app, kept in public/ as static assets available at runtime but not currently referenced by application logic.

nodes.label: icon.png
nodes.description: Next.js App Router convention-based app icon and favicon for the CYC Think Tank survey platform. A 630x630 pixel PNG image with RGBA color and alpha transparency (144 DPI). Automatically discovered by Next.js to serve as the application icon in browsers, bookmark bars, and mobile home screens.

nodes.label: CYC Think Tank Branding
nodes.description: Brand identity for the Canadian Youth Council survey platform, with the tagline 'Share your voice. Empower Canadian youth.' The icon represents the visual identity used across the application including favicons, PWA icons, and social sharing metadata.

nodes.label: Next.js icon.png Convention
nodes.description: Next.js App Router automatically recognizes icon.png (as well as icon.ico, icon.jpg, icon.jpeg, icon.svg) in the app/ directory as the application icon. It generates the appropriate <link> tags in the HTML head for favicons and apple-touch-icons at build time.

links.label: Layout metadata defines the brand title 'CYC Think Tank' and description
Next.js automatically detects icon.png alongside layout.tsx in the app/ directory and generates corresponding <link> tags in the document head
The icon visually represents the CYC Think Tank brand identity and serves as the platform's primary visual identifier
Placed at src/app/icon.png following the Next.js App Router convention for automatic icon/favicon resolution
